name: Quantum Gravity
description: Loop quantum gravity, string theory, holography, causal sets

results.name: Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy Formula
results.structural_conclusion: The entropy of a black hole is proportional to its surface area rather than volume, suggesting that quantum gravitational degrees of freedom are fundamentally holographic and encode information on boundaries rather than in bulk spacetime.

results.name: AdS/CFT Correspondence
results.structural_conclusion: A gravitational theory in anti-de Sitter space is exactly dual to a conformal field theory on its boundary, revealing that spacetime geometry and quantum entanglement are fundamentally equivalent descriptions of the same physics.
results.source_citation: Maldacena, J. (1998). Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231-252

results.name: Ryu-Takayanagi Formula
results.structural_conclusion: Entanglement entropy in the boundary theory equals the area of minimal surfaces in the bulk geometry, establishing that quantum information structure directly determines spacetime geometry.
results.source_citation: Ryu, S. & Takayanagi, T. (2006). Phys. Rev. Lett. 96, 181602

results.name: Loop Quantum Gravity Area Spectrum
results.structural_conclusion: Geometric area in quantum gravity has a discrete spectrum with minimum eigenvalue proportional to the Planck area, revealing that spacetime itself has an atomic structure at the fundamental scale.
results.source_citation: Rovelli, C. & Smolin, L. (1995). Nucl. Phys. B 442, 593-619

results.name: Black Hole Information Paradox Resolution
results.structural_conclusion: Information escapes black holes through entanglement between early and late Hawking radiation, requiring either firewalls at horizons or fundamental modifications to locality, revealing deep tension between unitarity and smooth spacetime.

results.name: Emergent Spacetime from Entanglement
results.structural_conclusion: Classical spacetime connectivity emerges from quantum entanglement patterns, with Einstein's equations arising as constraints on entanglement entropy, suggesting spacetime is not fundamental but emergent from quantum information.
results.source_citation: Van Raamsdonk, M. (2010). Gen. Rel. Grav. 42, 2323-2329

results.name: Causal Set Hauptvermutung
results.structural_conclusion: A spacetime manifold can be faithfully approximated by a discrete causal set whose elements represent spacetime events, with the continuum emerging only at large scales from fundamentally discrete causal structure.
results.source_citation: Bombelli, L. et al. (1987). Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 521-524

results.name: String Theory Dualities
results.structural_conclusion: Different string theories are equivalent under dualities like T-duality and S-duality, revealing that apparently distinct spacetime geometries and coupling regimes describe the same physical reality.
results.source_citation: Hull, C. & Townsend, P. (1995). Nucl. Phys. B 438, 109-137

results.name: Holographic Entanglement Entropy
results.structural_conclusion: Entanglement entropy in time-dependent states corresponds to extremal surfaces in bulk spacetime, generalizing Ryu-Takayanagi and showing that quantum information dynamics directly governs gravitational physics.

results.name: ER=EPR Conjecture
results.structural_conclusion: Einstein-Rosen bridges (wormholes) and Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen pairs (entanglement) are dual descriptions of the same phenomenon, unifying spacetime connectivity with quantum correlation.
results.source_citation: Maldacena, J. & Susskind, L. (2013). Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781-811

results.name: Spin Foam Models
results.structural_conclusion: Spacetime histories in quantum gravity can be represented as combinatorial structures (spin foams) that encode both geometry and matter, with classical spacetime emerging from superpositions of discrete quantum geometries.
results.source_citation: Reisenberger, M. & Rovelli, C. (1997). Class. Quant. Grav. 14, 1357-1371

results.name: Black Hole Thermodynamics Laws
results.structural_conclusion: Black holes obey laws precisely analogous to thermodynamics with temperature proportional to surface gravity and entropy to area, revealing that gravitational horizons are thermodynamic objects with microscopic quantum degrees of freedom.
results.source_citation: Bardeen, J. et al. (1973). Commun. Math. Phys. 31, 161-170

results.name: Quantum Error Correction in AdS/CFT
results.structural_conclusion: The holographic dictionary implements a quantum error-correcting code where bulk operators are protected against boundary erasures, revealing that spacetime emergence has built-in redundancy like quantum information protocols.

results.name: Gravitational Path Integral Measure
results.structural_conclusion: The Euclidean path integral for gravity requires summing over topologies and metrics modulo diffeomorphisms, but the measure and convergence remain problematic, indicating fundamental ambiguity in defining quantum superpositions of spacetimes.
results.source_citation: Gibbons, G. et al. (1978). Nucl. Phys. B 138, 141-150

results.name: Causal Dynamical Triangulations
results.structural_conclusion: Imposing causality constraints on discrete quantum geometries leads to emergence of a four-dimensional de Sitter universe at large scales, suggesting that macroscopic spacetime structure requires microscopic causal ordering.
results.source_citation: Ambjorn, J. et al. (2001). Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 924-927

results.name: Asymptotic Safety
results.structural_conclusion: Gravity may be renormalizable due to a non-Gaussian fixed point at high energies, implying that quantum gravity could be defined as a conventional quantum field theory with infinitely many couplings flowing to finite values.

structural_conclusion: Quantum gravity reveals that spacetime is not fundamental but emerges from quantum information structures, with geometric properties encoded holographically on boundaries rather than in bulk volumes. The discrete, quantum nature of geometry at the Planck scale, combined with dualities between entanglement and geometry, suggests that reality's fundamental layer consists of quantum information patterns from which both matter and spacetime arise as effective descriptions.
